jokes.text: We put a bunch of people on a plane, fly them over the Atlantic, then Tom Bergeron comes out and reveals that the pilot is a six year-old boy. We call it 'Child Hell Flight.'
jokes.setup: Pitching what sounds like a standard reality show format
jokes.punchline: Absurd reveal that the pilot is a child, followed by the terrible pun title
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity - starts normal, gets progressively darker. The title 'Child Hell Flight' is darkly hilarious wordplay. Pure 30 Rock DNA.

jokes.text: He is pitching a no-hitter!
jokes.setup: Jonathan leaving after his pitch
jokes.punchline: Baseball metaphor applied to business pitching
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth earnestness with clever double meaning. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Starting at 4:00 p.m. Yesterday, when Mr. Donaghy coined the word 'innoventually,' he has been flawless.
jokes.setup: Explaining Jack's perfect streak
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'innoventually' presented as brilliant coinage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack corporate-speak parody. The word sounds real but is obviously ridiculous. Kenneth's reverence sells it.

jokes.text: Because you have so many unsolvable problems. Like your mouth. It looks like someone kicked a hole in a bag of flour!
jokes.setup: Explaining why Lemon has to leave during the perfect streak
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly vicious visual insult from sweet Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Shocking meanness from Kenneth creates perfect contrast. The specific imagery is vivid and brutal. Character subversion at its finest.

jokes.text: 'Child Hell Flight' will innoventually be on the spring schedule.
jokes.setup: Jack using his made-up word
jokes.punchline: Callback to both the terrible show idea and the fake word
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces both jokes. Jack's casual use of his made-up word sells his confidence.

jokes.text: When you're pitching a perfect game, you don't walk Albert Pujols. And you are the Albert Pujols of having problems.
jokes.setup: Baseball metaphor for his perfect streak
jokes.punchline: Liz as the home run hitter of problems
jokes.explanation: Great extended metaphor that perfectly captures Liz's role as chaos agent. Jack's elaborate sports analogies are character-perfect.

jokes.text: And I'm including sex last night. Here's Avery's thank-you note.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming perfection in everything
jokes.punchline: Sex so clinical it gets a thank-you note
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. The idea of a thank-you note for sex is absurd and perfectly captures Jack and Avery's relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: No. But she respects it when it's done correctly.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Avery likes sex
jokes.punchline: Treating sex like a business presentation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant follow-up that doubles down on the corporate approach to intimacy. Jack's complete sincerity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Making it to a full 24 hours without a single misstep is called 'Reaganing'. The only other people who've ever done it? Lee Iacocca, Jack Welch, and, no judgment, Saddam Hussein.
jokes.setup: Explaining the term 'Reaganing' with business leaders
jokes.punchline: Casually including a dictator in the list
jokes.explanation: Dark humor masterpiece. The 'no judgment' qualifier makes it even funnier. Perfect Jack obliviousness to moral implications.

jokes.text: And I can't get a cab because Greece is playing Pakistan in soccer.
jokes.setup: Liz needing to get to the airport
jokes.punchline: Absurd reason for no cabs involving obscure international soccer
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist New York detail. The specificity of Greece vs Pakistan makes it funnier than generic soccer reference.

jokes.text: I have to talk to Rachel Maddow. Only one of us can have this haircut.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his trip to MSNBC
jokes.punchline: Territorial dispute over a haircut with a TV host
jokes.explanation: Brilliant vanity joke that's both topical and character-specific. The serious delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: My single is number four in Japan! Choke me, choke me, Blonde-like choke me
jokes.setup: Jenna being excluded from MTV awards
jokes.punchline: Terrible song lyrics delivered with pride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna delusion. The awful lyrics and her pride in Japanese success capture her character perfectly.

jokes.text: Because if I do, that judge is gonna make me join the Coast Guard.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming he can't turn down community service
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's court-ordered with an absurd alternative punishment
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy legal trouble escalation. Coast Guard as punishment is wonderfully specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Ice cream. Hey, don't you want some ice cream?
jokes.setup: Jenna's supposed honor from Carvel
jokes.punchline: Terrible commercial-style delivery for ice cream
jokes.explanation: Solid Jenna moment showing her terrible acting skills. The awkward delivery is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: If I want to see a 50-foot Spider-Man, I'll just go into my rec room, thank you.
jokes.setup: Jenna dismissing the Macy's parade
jokes.punchline: Implying she has a giant Spider-Man in her recreation room
jokes.explanation: Great wealth/excess joke that's perfectly Jenna. The casual delivery of something absurd works beautifully.

jokes.text: Well, one of the camera guys just had a baby, and I'm sick of hearing about it. This will put me back on top!
jokes.setup: Jenna buying cake for the crew
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's pure selfishness disguised as generosity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism. The idea of competing with a new baby for attention is beautifully self-centered.

jokes.text: When I see you chew your nails like that, it's either you're very anxious or you handled some ham earlier.
jokes.setup: Jack observing Liz's behavior
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific alternative explanation involving ham
jokes.explanation: Great weird Jack observation. The specificity of 'handled some ham' is perfectly random and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: A guy on crutches bit it in the revolving door. And he was so scared!
jokes.setup: Explaining why she and Carol were laughing
jokes.punchline: Finding humor in someone's accident and fear
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that shows Liz and Carol's shared twisted sense of humor. Character-revealing moment.

jokes.text: Well, I found my first gray toeknuckle hair!
jokes.setup: Jack challenging Liz about not talking about anything
jokes.punchline: Immediate overshare about gross personal detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz overshare that immediately proves Jack wrong. 'Toeknuckle' is a disgusting made-up body part that sounds real.

jokes.text: If I could press a button and five people in the world would die, but I'd get free cable for life, I'd do it.
jokes.setup: Continuation of oversharing examples
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to potential murder for cable TV
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that perfectly captures Liz's casual amorality and cheap priorities. The specific ratio makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And I'd been on the toilet so long that my legs had fallen asleep, so when I tried to stand, I just fell into my throw-up.
jokes.setup: More oversharing examples
jokes.punchline: Elaborate gross-out story with escalating disasters
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that's very Liz, but slightly relies on shock value. The escalation works well though.

jokes.text: You and I have never had an adult conversation about boning.
jokes.setup: Jack identifying what Liz won't talk about
jokes.punchline: Using juvenile term 'boning' in context of 'adult conversation'
jokes.explanation: Great contradiction between 'adult conversation' and 'boning.' Classic Jack attempting to be relatable.

jokes.text: I faced it myself with Greta Van Susteren before her head transplant.
jokes.setup: Jack relating to performance issues
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about Greta Van Susteren having a head transplant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist throwaway that treats plastic surgery like medical fact. Perfect Jack casual name-dropping with impossible twist.

jokes.text: I freaked out, and my junk closed for business. It's like Fort Knox down there.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her performance problem
jokes.punchline: Fort Knox metaphor for sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that's both crude and clever. Perfect Liz awkward honesty about sexual issues.

jokes.text: I'm just going to raise the barrier if that's okey-dokey with everybody.
jokes.setup: Liz's Fort Knox metaphor
jokes.punchline: Taxi driver immediately raising privacy barrier
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual reaction beat. The polite 'okey-dokey' makes the awkwardness even funnier. Great timing and escalation.

jokes.text: Scripts get in the way of my process, Shawn. Let's just shoot a hundred of these and see what we get.
jokes.setup: Director mentioning they faxed a script
jokes.punchline: Tracy's completely unprofessional approach to commercial filming
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy method acting parody. The waste of shooting 'a hundred' captures celebrity excess perfectly.

jokes.text: 'Jenny?' That's not my name! I don't want Wheelchair Jenny from Accounting getting credit for my cake!
jokes.setup: Jenna seeing the wrong name on the cake
jokes.punchline: Immediately identifying and dismissing a specific disabled coworker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism and casual cruelty. The specificity of 'Wheelchair Jenny' shows her petty workplace knowledge.

jokes.text: We've had to sell off Sally, Julie, and Poppy. Are those some of your pigs? Yes.
jokes.setup: Kenneth talking about selling off what sound like family members
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're actually pigs
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke that works because Kenneth would name pigs like family. Good setup and payoff structure.

jokes.text: You have more sexual hang-ups than an adult chat line run by Gilbert Gottfried.
jokes.setup: Jack analyzing Liz's issues
jokes.punchline: Specific, horrifying comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing image - Gilbert Gottfried's voice ruins everything sexual.

jokes.text: That was written by a computer program we're working on to replace you.
jokes.setup: Jack delivering an overly elaborate insult
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was computer-generated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about comedy writing itself. Self-referential humor that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Oh, that word bums me out unless it's between the words 'meat' and 'pizza.'
jokes.setup: Jack using the word 'lovers'
jokes.punchline: Liz only liking it in context of 'meat lovers pizza'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz food obsession joke with clever wordplay. Economy and character integration are flawless.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I'm a real woman and not some oversexed New York nympho like those sluts on 'Everybody Loves Raymond'.
jokes.setup: Liz defending her sexual attitudes
jokes.punchline: Calling the famously wholesome Raymond characters sluts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor calling the most wholesome sitcom characters sluts. Perfect misunderstanding of what constitutes sexual liberation.

jokes.text: Stop it, Jack! Stop it! Stop asking about the roller skates!
jokes.setup: Jack psychoanalyzing Liz's sexual issues
jokes.punchline: Revealing there's a specific roller skate trauma
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the major flashback sequence. Specific enough to be intriguing while maintaining mystery.

jokes.text: We're the luckiest people on earth! Now, someone get me a Jolt Cola! It does not exist anymore!
jokes.setup: Tracy celebrating their good fortune
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding something that no longer exists
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy out-of-touch celebrity moment. The immediate deflation from joy to disappointment works well.

jokes.text: She must have thought you meant credit card. How many cashiers are at that store? Two! This is fun!
jokes.setup: Kenneth getting cash back from Carvel
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately seeing scam potential, Kenneth's innocent enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the scam subplot. Kenneth's innocent 'This is fun!' contrasts perfectly with Jenna's criminal intent.

jokes.text: It's been years since my mom and I used to pull slip-and-falls at supermarkets.
jokes.setup: Jenna getting excited about the scam
jokes.punchline: Revealing a history of insurance fraud with her mother
jokes.explanation: Great character backstory that explains Jenna's criminal tendencies. Dark humor about insurance fraud delivered casually.

jokes.text: I don't understand what you're saying, but I like that it has the word 'we' in it!
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining the Arizona double-back scam
jokes.punchline: Kenneth focused only on being included rather than understanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence and desire for friendship. The focus on 'we' over the actual content is beautifully character-specific.

jokes.text: Yes, 'Bappy Hirthday, Gremlin'.
jokes.setup: Series of increasingly garbled cake orders
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical cake message delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the scam sequence. The progression to complete gibberish works well with Kenneth's earnest delivery.

jokes.text: Well, Liz, will you tell the Gipper your sexual story?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to help Liz open up
jokes.punchline: Full Reagan impersonation to discuss sexual trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that takes the Reagan obsession to absurd conclusion. Perfect Jack method for helping someone.

jokes.text: which everybody thought was a Dorothy Hamill but was actually a Pete Rose.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her childhood haircut
jokes.punchline: Comparing figure skater and baseball player hairstyles
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that shows Liz's childhood awkwardness. The contrast between feminine and masculine references works well.

jokes.text: Grizzly Adams. Larry Wilcox. Han Solo. Tug McGraw. Mike Schmidt. Kermit. Gunther Gebel-Williams! She took all the people away, Jack! Sex makes the people go away!
jokes.setup: Liz's trauma about her mother taking her posters
jokes.punchline: Eclectic list of childhood crushes leading to sexual revelation
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation from childhood trauma to adult insight. The bizarre mix of celebrities and the final revelation is perfectly crafted. Peak 30 Rock writing.

jokes.text: Adding brick after brick to our friendship castle so it someday reaches the sky!
jokes.setup: Kenneth thinking they're just talking
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor for friendship building
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocent romanticism about friendship. The castle metaphor is beautifully earnest and naive.

jokes.text: If Cookie Puss knew, he'd tear us apart with his fangs!
jokes.setup: Kenneth worried about their scam being wrong
jokes.punchline: Treating Carvel mascot as real threat with fangs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth literal thinking about corporate mascots. The specific detail of fangs makes it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And with enough money, they could buy those magic beans from that old hermit. We'd be rich!
jokes.setup: Kenneth thinking about helping his family
jokes.punchline: Believing in literal fairy tale economics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence treating fairy tales as investment advice. Character-specific magical thinking.

jokes.text: I am the Sisyphus of Reaganing.
jokes.setup: Jack frustrated with Tracy's problems
jokes.punchline: Comparing his situation to Greek mythology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack pretension combined with accurate metaphor. Classical reference that actually fits the situation perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's the kind of stuff I should be saying!
jokes.setup: Jack's eloquent Sisyphus reference
jokes.punchline: Tracy recognizing it as the type of smart thing he should say
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Tracy recognizes good writing. Self-aware humor about the show's own wit levels.

jokes.text: how they would use jellybeans to convince people that he was...
jokes.setup: Jack telling story about Reagan's mental decline
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about using props to hide dementia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about Reagan's decline that Jack would never consciously acknowledge. The trailing off makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm sending you some money so you can get your operation. Now can you put a human on the phone?
jokes.setup: Jack on the phone being helpful
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been talking to an automated system
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Jack's inability to distinguish automated systems. Character-appropriate technological confusion.

jokes.text: Anybody gets hurt during the score, we leave them behind to die.
jokes.setup: Jenna agreeing to one last con
jokes.punchline: Establishing needlessly brutal rules for ice cream scam
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of criminal movie tropes applied to petty ice cream fraud. Jenna's casual cruelty is beautifully overblown.

jokes.text: Isn't it 'Frasier'? No, it's 'Frajer'. And I should know. I'm Frajer.
jokes.setup: Employee questioning the cake spelling
jokes.punchline: Kenneth insisting on wrong spelling and claiming to be Frajer
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the scam with Kenneth's earnest commitment to the lie. The mispronunciation and identity claim work perfectly.

jokes.text: I should throw some tossed salad and scrambled eggs at you! Frajer!
jokes.setup: Employee frustrated with Kenneth's Frajer claim
jokes.punchline: Reference to Frasier theme song delivered angrily
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the employee knows what Kenneth is doing. Theme song reference is nicely specific.

jokes.text: $800 split three ways. Those ice cream saps will rue the day they ever tangled with... the Best Friends Gang!
jokes.setup: Jenna celebrating their successful scam
jokes.punchline: Dramatic gang name that's completely inappropriate for the crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between criminal movie tropes and wholesome gang name. Jenna's dramatic delivery sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's $20 for a party, $60 to bite me during it. I'll do stuff with a dog, but I get to pick the dog.
jokes.setup: Prostitute offering services
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and bizarre menu of options
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor but relies somewhat on shock value. The dog specification is absurdly specific in a funny way.

jokes.text: You two are pretty uptight for hanging out under a bridge.
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz being awkward about sex
jokes.punchline: Prostitute pointing out the irony of their location vs. behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the contrast between their prudishness and environment. Well-delivered reality check.

jokes.text: I just got fired 'cause Kelsey Grammer scammed me!
jokes.setup: Employee explaining why she was fired
jokes.punchline: Revealing she thinks Kenneth was actually Kelsey Grammer
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reveals the employee actually believed Kenneth's lie. Perfect escalation of the scam consequences.

jokes.text: Through seduction!
jokes.setup: Jenna describing new con method
jokes.punchline: Sexual angle to medical fraud
jokes.explanation: Fits Jenna's character but feels like predictable escalation.

jokes.text: I hope you get into a car accident someday so I can have it.
jokes.setup: Jenna complimenting Kenneth's good heart
jokes.punchline: Immediately wishing him harm to benefit herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment that starts sweet and immediately turns selfish and dark. The casual cruelty is beautifully character-specific.

jokes.text: In certain lights you're an eight using East Coast over-35 standards excluding Miami.
jokes.setup: Jack complimenting Liz
jokes.punchline: Extremely qualified and specific rating system
jokes.explanation: Masterful Jack compliment that's both sweet and completely analytical. The specific exclusions and qualifiers are perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: But if I couldn't get it done in Vegas after a Penn and Teller show, I don't know how it's going to happen here.
jokes.setup: Liz doubting she can overcome her sexual issues
jokes.punchline: Implication that Penn and Teller show should have been sexually inspiring
jokes.explanation: Great Liz logic that Penn and Teller would be peak romance. Perfect character-specific weird association.

jokes.text: Yes! When this happened with Joel, 'What's new, pussycat?' was playing on the bus we were on... I mean, the room we were in!
jokes.setup: Liz remembering pattern
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals having sex on bus
jokes.explanation: Good callback with additional character revelation through slip-up.

jokes.text: I appreciate that, Lemon, but if you ever speak ill of Reagan again, I will smack those teeth straight.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Jack is better than Reagan
jokes.punchline: Threat of violence for potential Reagan criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Reagan worship taken to violent extreme. The teeth threat is specifically brutal and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, God no! You've got years of therapy ahead of you. Probably electroshock.
jokes.setup: Liz asks if she's fixed
jokes.punchline: Casually predicting extensive treatment
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of hope with Jack's brutal honesty about therapy needs.

jokes.text: I appreciate that, Lemon, but if you ever speak ill of Reagan again, I will smack those teeth straight.
jokes.setup: Liz says Jack is better than Reagan
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence for political heresy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - political devotion overriding personal gratitude.

jokes.text: He's at JFK! I wrote it down wrong!
jokes.setup: All this effort to get to Newark
jokes.punchline: Carol is at completely different airport
jokes.explanation: Good classic sitcom misunderstanding payoff. Perfect Liz incompetence that undermines the entire journey.

jokes.text: I have been sexually rejected by not one, but two guys who later went to clown college.
jokes.setup: Liz sharing sexual failures
jokes.punchline: Multiple rejections from future clowns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz romantic failure escalation. The specificity of clown college makes it even more pathetic and funny.

jokes.text: And one time in summer camp I kissed a girl on a dare, but then she drowned.
jokes.setup: Liz continuing to share sexual history
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of death following romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor delivered casually. The connection between the kiss and drowning is perfectly absurd and morbid.
